title: LAMP/RT-LAMP COVID positive control
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"fcj5v","text":"This protocol explains how to prepare dsDNA and ssRNA target positive control for LAMP and RT-LAMP reactions. Particularly it describes the pipeline for SARS-Cov-2 Nucleocapsid (N) gene positive control which is included in the ReClone collection. The general procedure involves amplifying the gene of interest with primers which add T7 promoter and T7 terminator to its sequence followed by T7 in vitro transcription to obtain the ssRNA.  ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2a9g1","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"e7r3g","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ar5t6","text":" ","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"a15hj","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2etsg","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"927vk","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8nk2o","text":"Sequence elaboration:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":21}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"62psq","text":"Sequence OrfN_5 was designed and synthesized based on IDT SARS-CoV-2 positive control plasmid (CAT#10006625_2019).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"UNDERLINE","offset":9,"length":6},{"style":"UNDERLINE","offset":95,"length":17}],"entityRanges":[{"key":1,"offset":9,"length":6},{"key":2,"offset":95,"length":17}],"data":{}},{"key":"6lpj7","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7hnb4","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4p1h3","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"image","mutability":"","data":{"guid":"61D8D43F341F11EE9D4302C0B41BC903","height":601,"is_video":false,"legend":"{\"blocks\":[{\"key\":\"ca7et\",\"text\":\"Figure 1: General pipeline. It shows how dsDNA is amplified from control plasmid at the time pT7 and tT7 are incorporated to its sequence which is used to perform the ssRNA T7 in vitro transcription.\",\"type\":\"unstyled\",\"depth\":0,\"inlineStyleRanges\":[{\"style\":\"bold\",\"offset\":0,\"length\":8}],\"entityRanges\":[],\"data\":{}}],\"entityMap\":{}}","mime":"image/png","original_name":"positive_control.png","placeholder":"https://content.protocols.io/files/c3rghefe.jpg","source":"https://content.protocols.io/files/c3rehefe.jpg","type":1,"webp_placeholder":"https://s3.amazonaws.com/protocols-files/files/c3rfhefe.webp","webp_source":"https://content.protocols.io/files/c3rdhefe.webp","width":550}},"1":{"type":"link","mutability":"","data":{"guid":"A6BBACFF739211EBA67A0A58A9FEAC2A","url":"https://benchling.com/s/seq-75ss3ORzeIIdAdEoeXd8"}},"2":{"type":"link","mutability":"","data":{"guid":"A6BBAD5D739211EBA67A0A58A9FEAC2A","url":"https://benchling.com/s/seq-p10uFkf1oL5q76J5ym96"}}}}

steps: Amplify SARS-Cov-2 Nucleocapsid (N) gene fragment dsDNA from ReClone or IDT positive control plasmid with the ultramers indicated in Table 1. Table 1 : Primers/Ultramers used to amplify N-gene positive control The amplification can be carried out using any standard PCR reaction formulation (e.g. Phusion DNA polymerase with HF buffer), adding 0.01 ng to 1 ng of the plasmid template and 1 µL of each primer at 10 ?M concentration and following this thermocycler program: Initial denaturation at 98 °C for 00:00:30 Followed by 35 cycles of: 98 °C per 00:00:05 57 °C per 00:00:15 extension at 72 °C per 00:01:00 End by a final extension at 72 °C per 00:05:00
dsDNA purification Perform PCR reaction purification with any commercially available kit. We use Promega Wizard® SV Gel and PCR Clean-Up System Typical yields are around 25 µL at 30 - 100 ng/?L.

In vitro transcription preparation In vitro transcription has to be done from previously prepared dsDNA. We do it using HiScribe™ ( NEB E2040S ) system plus PPI ( NEB M0361S ) and RNasin® ( Promega N2111 ): Table 2: In vitro transcription formulation.
Treat the reaction with DNAse I ( NEB M0303S ) by diluting the reaction with 70 µL H 2 0 (molecular grade) and 10 µL 10X buffer DNAse I.
Incubated at 37 °C per 00:15:00 . Heat inactivation of DNAse is not recommended because it can harm RNA.
Perform RNA purification with any available kit. We use Qiagen RNeasy Kit . Typical yields are around 50 µL at 1000ng/?L.

Compute the sample copy number concentration [copies/?L] by replacing the length [bp] ( Table 3 ) and mass concentration [ng/?L] (C_{mass}) in the next mathematical expressions : (1.a) \text{Z}\frac{g}{\operatorname{mol} d s D N A}=\text { length } d s D N A[b p] * 617.96 \frac{g}{m o l * b p}+36.04 \frac{g}{m o l} (1.b) \text{Z}\frac{g}{\operatorname{mol} ssR N A}=\text { length } s s R N A[b p] * 321.47 \frac{g}{m o l * b p}+18.02 \frac{g}{m o l} (2) \text{Y}\frac{\text { copies }}{n g}=\left(\text{Z}\frac{g}{\text { mol } ss R N A} * \frac{10^{9} n g}{g}\right)^{-1} * 6,02210^{23} \frac{\text { copies }}{\operatorname{mol} d s D N A} (3) \text{X}\frac{\text { copies }}{\mu L}=C_{mass}\frac{n g}{\mu L} * \text{Y}\frac{\text { copies }}{n g} Table 3: dsDNA and ssRNA fragment sizes. use \text{eq. 1.a} for dsDNA and \text{eq. 1.b} fo for ssRNA. Typilcall amounts are: dsDNA: 10 8 - 10 9 copies/ng ssRNA: 10 9 - 10 10 copies/ng

Make serial dilutions with molecular grade H 2 O to reach the target \frac{\text { copies }}{\mu L} concentration. As positive control we typically use 1 µL of sample at \text {10.000 }\frac{\text { copies }}{\mu L} You can store aliquots of dsDNA samples at targets concentrations at -20 °C or -80 °C . Freezing and thawing cycles may affect the concentration then use each aliquot just one time.

In order to compute the sample copy number concentration , it's necessary to measure its mass concentration in a nano-spectrophotometer, plate reader, or estimate it by gel electrophoresis imaging. NanoDrop™ 3300 Fluorospectrometer (Fluorospectrometer, NanoDrop™ 3300, nd-3300, https://www.thermofisher.com/order/catalog/product/ND-3300#/ND-3300 , https://s3.amazonaws.com/protocols-files/public/0a4fe9b7a11f73c8ba5166c11a7060d8420e56909f0bef279aef656fabd5f2ab/btsr9xr6.jpg ) Repeat steps 8 to 10 for ssRNA each time you perform experimentation because samples and aliquots concentration is not stable even though keeping them frozen.
Store sample under -20 °C In case you are not making RT-LAMP positive control go to step 10 .
Perform aliquots and store the samples at -80 °C
creator: Tamara Matute
